'''Kenny Edwards''' (born 13 September 1989) is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}}.

He previously played for the [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[NRL]], and the [[Catalans Dragons]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]]. Edwards has also spent time on loan at [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].

==Early life==
Edwards was born in [[Levin, New Zealand]], and is of [[Māori people|Māori]] and [[Cook Islander|Cook Island]] descent.{{fact|date=May 2022}}

He played his junior football for the Levin Knights and the Pukekohe Pythons, before being signed by the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]].{{fact|date=May 2022}}

As a youngster, Edwards played for the [[Junior Kiwis]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/parramatta-eels-dock-kenny-edwards-second-game-20140802-zzpsd.html|title=Parramatta Eels dock Kenny Edwards second game|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=2 August 2014|access-date=8 January 2015}}</ref>

In 2007, Edwards played for the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]].<ref name="1eyedeel.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.1eyedeel.com/forum/topics/eels-sign-another-bad-boy-reserve-grader|title=Eels sign another bad-boy reserve grader...|date=11 October 2012|publisher=Leyedeel.com|access-date=8 January 2015}}</ref>

==Club career==
In 2008, Edwards played for [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]]' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ourfootyteam.com/nswrl_toyotaCup.php|title=TOYOTA CUP TRIALS RAIDERS READY TO TACKLE TIGERS - LUKE SET TO STAR IN MOLLYMOOK CLASH|publisher=Ourfootyteam.com|access-date=8 January 2015}}</ref> however his contract was terminated at the start of 2009 due to disciplinary reasons.

He instead joined the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] for the 2009 season, again playing in the NYC competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ourfootyteam.com/keebraPark/keebra_park_2010_archives.php|title=LATEST KEEBRA PARK FOOTY NEWS|publisher=Ourfootyteam.com|access-date=8 January 2015}}</ref> Later on, he had his contract terminated once again for disciplinary reasons which forced him to sit out the 2011 season.<ref name="1eyedeel.com"/>

In 2012 Edwards joined the [[Southport Tigers]] in the [[Gold Coast Rugby League]] competition. After acting as a trainer in one of the Tigers' under-17s matches, Edwards was accused of repeatedly inciting his team to fight their opponents. He was subsequently banned for the rest of the season, but had the charges downgraded on appeal and was allowed to play for Southport in the final series.<ref name="1eyedeel.com"/>

===Parramatta Eels===
Edwards joined the [[Parramatta Eels]] on a one-year contract for the 2013 season.<ref name="1eyedeel.com"/> On 25 July 2013, the contract was extended for another two seasons.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2013/07/25/edwards-re-signs-with-eels.html|title=Edwards Re-Signs with Eels|date=25 July 2013|publisher=Parraeels.com.au|access-date=8 January 2015}}</ref> He then made his NRL debut for the Eels during round 26 of the [[2013 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2013/round-26/newcastle-vs-parramatta/summary.html|title=NRL 2013|author=Shawn Dollin and Andrew Ferguson|date=16 October 2014|publisher=Rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=8 January 2015}}</ref>

On 22 September 2013, Edwards was named at second-row in the 2013 [[New South Wales Cup]] team of the year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2013/09/22/2013-nswrlcomau-vb-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year-announcement.html|title=2013 NSWRL.com.au VB NSW Cup Team of the Year - Announcement|publisher=Nswrl.com.au|access-date=8 January 2015|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150108222849/http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2013/09/22/2013-nswrlcomau-vb-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year-announcement.html|archive-date=8 January 2015}}</ref>

In October 2014, Edwards was named in the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] squad to play the [[Queensland]] [[Murri people|Murri]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2014/10/13/eels_named_for_nz_maori.html|title=Eels Named for NZ Maori|date=13 October 2014|publisher=Parraeels.com.au|access-date=8 January 2015}}</ref>

On 5 June 2014, Edwards again extended his contract with the Eels, signing until the end of 2017.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2014/06/05/eels_enforcers_re_sign_with_parramatta.html|title=Eels Enforcers Re-Sign with Parramatta|date=5 June 2014|publisher=Parraeels.com.au|access-date=8 January 2015}}</ref>

During the 2015 NRL season, Edwards was handed a 9 month playing ban and missed the entire season for taking a urine sample on behalf of fellow Parramatta player [[Kaysa Pritchard]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/eels/nrl-2016-parramatta-eels-hooker-kaysa-pritchard-wants-to-repay-the-faith-shown-by-coach-brad-arthur/news-story/063f5ddba51257188844e7fc0180fc01|title = NRL 2016: Parramatta Eels hooker Kaysa Pritchard wants to repay the faith shown by coach Brad Arthur|date = 16 March 2016}}</ref>

In 2017, Edwards missed the first 7 rounds of the season after pleading guilty to a domestic violence charge, having poured alcohol over the head of his partner during an argument. In his second game back, Edwards was the first player to be sinbinned after the NRL changed the rules to outlaw players slapping opposition players. Edwards was shown throwing a chair across the change rooms after the incident.<ref>{{cite web| work= nine.com.au | title=Video: Kenny Edwards the first NRL player to be sin binned for slap after Gavin Cooper scuffle| author=Paul Zalunardo | url=http://wwos.nine.com.au/2017/04/28/22/08/parramatta-eels-forward-kenny-edwards-falls-foul-of-nrl-crackdown-on-slapping}}</ref> Kenny Edwards played his 50th NRL match against the St George Illawarra Dragons in round 15 of the [[2017 NRL season]].

On 28 July 2017, during Parramatta's victory over Brisbane, Edwards delivered a late tackle on Brisbane player [[Jonus Pearson]], who was lying on the ground following a high tackle by Parramatta player [[Nathan Brown (rugby league, born 1993)|Nathan Brown]]. Edwards was later cited over the incident and faced a $1500 fine if he entered an early guilty plea.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/nrl-parramatta-eels-kenny-edwards-cops-fine-for-shot-on-brisbane-broncos-jonus-pearson-20170729-gxlgd7.html |accessdate=19 November 2021 |title=NRL: Parramatta Eels' Kenny Edwards cops fine for shot on Brisbane Broncos' Jonus Pearson |website=The Sydney Morning Herald | date=29 July 2017 | author1=Steve Zemek | author2=Scott Bailey }}</ref>

On 15 May 2018, Edwards was stood down indefinitely by Parramatta after he allegedly fled from police following a random roadside check in Merrylands, Sydney. Edwards did not face Fairfield Local Court but attended a traffic offenders course, having been accused of abandoning a vehicle he was driving on a suspended licence.  Edwards only raised the matter with the club on  7 May 2018, which was three weeks after allegedly running away from police after being approached by officers.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/05/15/kenny-edwards-stood-down-indefinitely-by-parramatta-eels/|title=Edwards stood down indefinitely by Eels|date=15 May 2018}}</ref>

On 19 May 2018, Edwards was released by Parramatta.  In his time at the club, Edwards missed over 35 games for Parramatta due to incidents off the field.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/kenny-edwards-parramatta-eels-released-contract-traffic-incident/6a195nexegpo1nhta3vvcofdo |title=Kenny Edwards officially released from Parramatta Eels {{!}} League {{!}} Sporting News |website=www.sportingnews.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180521191752/http://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/kenny-edwards-parramatta-eels-released-contract-traffic-incident/6a195nexegpo1nhta3vvcofdo |archive-date=2018-05-21}} </ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
On 25 May 2018, Edwards signed a one-and-a-half-year deal to join Super League side [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12216/11385436/catalans-dragons-sign-kenny-edwards-following-parramatta-exit|title = Catalans Dragons sign Kenny Edwards following Parramatta exit}}</ref>

In August 2018, Edwards was part of the Catalans Dragons side which won their first ever trophy, claiming victory in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/45007844|title=Relive Catalans' historic Challenge Cup final win|work=BBC Sport|date=19 August 2018}}</ref>

On 29 October 2018, Edwards was suspended from driving for 6 months and fined $600 for the incident which occurred back in May 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.9news.com.au/2018/10/29/17/11/kenny-edwards-nrl-player-disqualified-from-driving-court|title = Kenny Edwards sentenced over 'appalling' driving record}}</ref>

In the [[Super League XXIV]] season during a match between Catalans and [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], Edwards was involved in an ugly on-field brawl with opposition players.  The game was ultimately called off.  The fighting on the field also spilled off the field with rivals fans clashing in the stands.  Edwards was later charged by the Rugby Football League with crowd incitement but was then cleared of any wrongdoing.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2019/aug/06/catalans-dragons-kenny-edwards-charged-incitement-warrington/|title=Catalans' Kenny Edwards cleared of incitement charge after crowd trouble|website=The Guardian|date=6 August 2019}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
In November 2019, it was announced that Edwards had signed a contract to join [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-sign-nrl-star-17327239/|title=Huddersfield Giants sign NRL star on two year deal|website=Examiner Live|access-date=2 December 2019|archive-date=13 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210613094634/https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-sign-nrl-star-17327239|url-status=dead}}</ref>

On 7 July 2021, Edwards was suspended for ten matches after pleading guilty to a charge of Other Contrary Behaviour.  Edwards was initially placed on report during Huddersfield's game against [[Catalans Dragons]] after appearing to stick his finger in [[Sam Kasiano]]'s buttocks. Edwards was also fined £500.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leaguewrites.com/2021/07/07/edwards-given-10-match-ban/?fbclid=IwAR2v_8Kgsm-PMd0dUZIugMwnEWeZG19FWbDEEypdqEvQqiYt7aA_GADJJ7Q/|title=Edwards given 10-match ban|website=leaguewrites.com|date=7 July 2021|access-date=7 July 2021|archive-date=9 July 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210709184600/https://leaguewrites.com/2021/07/07/edwards-given-10-match-ban/?fbclid=IwAR2v_8Kgsm-PMd0dUZIugMwnEWeZG19FWbDEEypdqEvQqiYt7aA_GADJJ7Q/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers (loan)===
On 19 September 2021, it was reported that Edwards had signed for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] on a season-long loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=19 Sep 2021|accessdate=31 Dec 2021|title=Kenny Edwards: Castleford Tigers sign Huddersfield Giants back-rower on loan for 2022|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58604829}}</ref> He made his Tigers debut on 11 February against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-02-11 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 16-26 Salford Red Devils |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/60320580 |access-date=2022-04-15 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He was voted the Fans' Man of the Match for his performance against the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Challenge Cup]],<ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=1508773123625213958|title=You chose Kenny Edwards as the @HuntersHomes Fans Man of the Match following our win over Leeds on Saturday!}}</ref> and scored his first try for the club on 14 April against [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Newsum |first=Matt |date=2022-04-14 |title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 4-34 Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61113166 |access-date=2022-04-15 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 24 June 2022, Edwards signed a three-year deal with Castleford to remain at the club until the end of 2025.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/kenny-edwards-club-for-2023-revealed-after-super-league-and-nrl-interest-54944/|title=Kenny Edwards’ club for 2023 revealed after Super League and NRL interest|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com}}</ref>
Edwards played 25 games for Castleford in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 11th on the table narrowly avoiding relegation.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-ace-alex-mellor-delivers-brutally-honest-assessment-of-awful-season-weve-massively/|title=Castleford Tigers ace Alex Mellor delivers brutally honest assessment of ‘awful’ season; ‘We’ve massively underperformed’|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===Mackay Cutters===
On 2 November 2023 it was reported that Edwards signed for the [[Mackay Cutters]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=2 Nov 2023|title=Kenny Edwards finds new club in Australia on two-year deal following Castleford Tigers departure|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/kenny-edwards-finds-new-club-in-australia-on-two-year-deal-following-castleford-tigers-departure}}</ref>

On 17 April 2024 he announced his retirement from playing to continue with his rugby league academy<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=17 April 2024|title=Ex-Castleford Tigers star Kenny Edwards exits rugby league for new position|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-castleford-tigers-star-kenny-edwards-exits-rugby-league-for-new-position/}}</ref>

===Representative===
On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]] he played for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] in their 26-24 victory over [[England national rugby league team|England]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England’s [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation.

==Personal life==
Edwards is the cousin of [[Wests Tigers]] player [[James Tamou]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/edwards-making-up-for-lost-time-in-nrl/tabid/10874/newsid/76751/default.aspx|title=Edwards Making Up For Lost Time in NRL|publisher=Nswrl.com.au|access-date=8 January 2015|archive-date=8 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150108222928/http://www.nrl.com/edwards-making-up-for-lost-time-in-nrl/tabid/10874/newsid/76751/default.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref> He has two children, a daughter named Malia-Rose Edwards and a son named Kymani Edwards.
